body.title: Validate end-to-end: worker=igor ticket processes via Qwen in-process
body.result: All 4 prereqs verified end-to-end: dispatch routed to igor branch (not konsole), goal_adopt succeeded, pe_chain fired via MCPCALL registry, pe_test pre-flight escalated correctly on pre-existing test_memory_scope failure. Commit 744157fa. Productive igor work blocked by T-fix-memory-scope-portable-test (filed).
body.description: After the four prior tickets land, validate the full in-process path end-to-end. Seed a deliberately simple test ticket (e.g., 'append then remove a test-only line in a scratch file'), trigger foreman (foreman_scan or BOREDOM_DETECTED), observe: did adopt_next_ticket fire (not launch_next_worker)? did Qwen do the reasoning (not Claude)? did the engram chain walk without WARN-and-noop? did the ticket close with a channel post? If the chain breaks anywhere: capture the specific failure, file a new ticket for the bug, gate this one on the bug's completion. Don't fix unrelated issues in passing. Scope: one validation run. Does NOT flip the default worker field — tickets still default to worker=claude, igor path remains opt-in pending validation success. Flipping the default is a follow-up decision. Test plan: this ticket IS the test. Success = one clean end-to-end run on Qwen, model logged in turn_trace, closure posted to channel. Failure = a well-scoped child ticket capturing the specific breakage.
